David Holub
Senior Data Scientist · Prague, Czech Republic
david.holub@example.cz · +420 602 778 119 · linkedin.com/in/davidholubds
Summary
Senior Data Scientist with 10 years of experience in Czech banking and insurance: credit-risk,
survival analysis, and model-explainability for regulator-facing reports. Currently leads a
4-person DS team at Komerční banka.
Experience
Senior Data Scientist — Komerční banka, Prague
March 2021 – present (5 years)
Leads a 4-person Data Science team owning the SME credit-risk scorecard programme.
Migrated the legacy logistic scorecard to a LightGBM 3.3 stack with SHAP explanations,
retaining model interpretability for the CNB regulator while lifting AUC from 0.72 to 0.79.
Built the survival-analysis model for early-warning signals on the SME portfolio (lifelines 0.27,
Cox PH), flagging at-risk accounts 38 days earlier on average than the previous rules-based
system.
Owns the model-documentation template the risk-management team submits to the regulator;
co-authored with the chief risk officer.
Data Scientist — Generali Česká, Prague
September 2017 – February 2021
Built the motor-insurance pricing model (GLM + GBM ensemble, scikit-learn 0.24) for the
Czech retail book; improved combined ratio by 2.1 pp over the prior pricing engine on a
12-month holdout.
Mentored 2 junior data scientists.
Junior Data Scientist — Generali Česká, Prague
October 2015 – August 2017
Built the fraud-detection rule-mining tooling on top of PostgreSQL; flagged a recurring
staged-collision pattern that recovered 4.2 MCZK in disallowed claims in 2016.
Education
Ing. (M.Sc.) in Statistics and Econometrics — VŠE Prague
2013 – 2015, thesis on survival analysis for retail credit.
Bc. (B.Sc.) in Quantitative Methods — VŠE Prague
2010 – 2013.
Skills
Python 3.11, scikit-learn 1.5, LightGBM 3.3, XGBoost 1.7, SHAP 0.45, lifelines 0.27,
statsmodels 0.14, PostgreSQL, SAS (legacy), Git.
Leadership: team lead (4 DS), regulator-facing documentation, model-risk-management
partnership.
Languages: Czech (native), English (C1), German (A2).
Selected projects

David Holub
Senior Data Scientist · Prague, Czech Republic
david.holub@example.cz · +420 602 778 119 · linkedin.com/in/davidholubds
SME scorecard migration (KB, 2023) — see Experience; AUC 0.72 → 0.79 with
SHAP-based regulator documentation.
Early-warning survival model (KB, 2024) — 38-day lead time over the rules-based
baseline.
